exercises.title: Logical Pauli Calculator
exercises.description: Calculate logical Pauli operators for various codes.
tasks: Given code stabilizers, find X_L and Z_L
Compute logical Y_L = iX_L Z_L
Verify X_L and Z_L anticommute
Check that logical Paulis commute with all stabilizers
Test for Steane, [[5,1,3]], and surface codes
hints: X_L must anticommute with Z stabilizers
Z_L must anticommute with X stabilizers
Logical operators commute with all stabilizers
For CSS codes, X_L and Z_L have simple forms

exercises.title: Gate Classification Tool
exercises.description: Build a tool to classify gates as Pauli, Clifford, or non-Clifford.
tasks: Implement Pauli gate check (is P² = I?)
Implement Clifford check (does G map Paulis to Paulis?)
Test common gates: X, Y, Z, H, S, T, CNOT
Classify arbitrary single-qubit unitaries
Identify which gates require magic states
hints: Clifford: check G X G† and G Z G† are Paulis
Non-Clifford: G X G† or G Z G† not Pauli

exercises.title: Fault Tolerance Analyzer
exercises.description: Analyze whether gate implementations are fault-tolerant.
tasks: Define fault-tolerance criteria
Given gate circuit, trace error propagation
Count errors per output block after single fault
Compare transversal vs non-transversal implementations
Output: FT-safe or FT-violation report
hints: Track how X errors propagate through CNOTs
Count maximum errors in each output block
Transversal = each qubit only touches one qubit
FT-safe: ≤1 error per block from single fault

exercises.title: Code Gate Support Matrix
exercises.description: Build a matrix showing which gates each code supports transversally.
tasks: Input: list of codes with their stabilizers
For each code, check transversal X, Z, H, S, CNOT, T
Verify by checking stabilizer preservation
Generate support matrix visualization
Identify complementary code pairs for switching
hints: G transversal iff G^⊗n S (G†)^⊗n = S for all stabilizers
S transforms some X stabilizers to Y
Find codes where one has T, other has S
